input.background: Suppose we want to identify the values of a $t$-random variable with 9 degrees of freedom that captures the middle $80\%$ of values on $t_9$. Start by sketching the curve, which is bell shaped with a mean of 0 and inflection points approximately one standard deviation above and below the mean.
input.question: Because the curve is symmetrical, we look for the critical values in Table C that cut off the bottom 10\% and top 10\% of the curve. These are the 10th percentile and 90th percentile of the $t_9$ distribution. What are the critical values?

output.answer: Table C lets us know that $t_{9,90}=1.383$. The symmetrical point on the left-hand aspect of the curve corresponds to $t_{9,10}=-1.383$.
